creator_registry_index.source_policy_posture: Static creator-registry index generated from the public-handle linkage ledger. It is a Discovery/Capture dedupe and routing surface over known public platform accounts, not metric authority, not raw capture storage, and not final cross-platform identity proof.

source_inputs.role: source-backed known public platform accounts and linkage state

lookup_policy.handle_mutability_note: Handles are mutable labels; prefer platform-native account ids when present.
lookup_policy.discovery_preflight_rule: Repeat observations of known accounts are preserved as signal but must attach to the existing registry row rather than open duplicate creator rows.
lookup_policy.capture_preflight_rule: Capture should use registry freshness/routing state to avoid duplicate work, then rely on Capture/Silver records for facts and metrics.

accepted_residuals: Current registry rows are platform-account rows from source-backed YouTube and Instagram seeds; TikTok rows are not admitted yet.
Discovery repeat-observation events are not yet stored as their own append-only event family.
Capture freshness is a routing hint copied from current static source-backed rows, not a live recency SLA.
Candidate or promoted cross-platform creator records remain absent until public-handle linkage evidence is admitted.
